sanskrit: युक्तः कर्म-फलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिम आप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम्। अयुक्तः काम-कारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते॥
transliteration: yuktaḥ karma-phalaṃ tyaktvā śāntim āpnoti naiṣṭhikīm | ayuktaḥ kāma-kāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate ||
literal_meaning: The integrated one, having relinquished the fruit of action, attains unshakable peace; the un-integrated, driven by desire, clings to results and becomes bound.
contextual_meaning: Peace is not the absence of work but the absence of self-stake in its outcome; attachment to results keeps the mind in a perpetual feedback loop of hope and fear.
psychological_state: Secure-functioning mindset: action is taken, but self-worth is not mortgaged to the result; cortisol baseline drops, prefrontal cortex stays online.
trigger_scenario: Refreshing your email every 30 seconds after a job interview, heart racing with each new message—versus submitting the application, closing the laptop, and going for a run.
anchor_text: Work with love, then let go; peace lives in the release.
